rules: All objects must exist in entities
Segment AB requires A and B in points
Plane ABC requires A, B, C in points
Objects in facts must be declared in entities
Angles defined by 3 vertices like 'BAD' MUST be converted to angle_type 'line_line'. The objects array MUST contain exactly 2 corresponding lines/segments (e.g., ['AB', 'AD'] for angle BAD).
NEVER use 'vertex' as an angle_type! It is NOT supported in the schema.

PARALLEL / PERPENDICULAR: data.objects MUST contain exactly 2 items. Use two segment names for line-line (e.g. ['AB', 'CD']), segment then plane for line-plane (e.g. ['AB', 'CDE']), two plane names for plane-plane (e.g. ['ABC', 'DEF']).
RAY: data requires point (new point on the ray), origin (ray origin), ray_point (second point defining direction). All three names must appear in entities.points.
OPPOSITE_RAY: same fields as ray (point, origin, ray_point). The new point lies on the ray opposite to origin-ray_point.
PERPENDICULAR_RAY: data requires point, origin, perpendicular_to. perpendicular_to is a segment name (line-line) or plane name (line-plane).
ANGLE_BISECTOR: data requires point, vertex, ray_1, ray_2. ray_1 and ray_2 are POINT names on the two sides of the angle, NOT segment or ray names. For angle BAC use vertex='A', ray_1='B', ray_2='C'.
CENTROID: use data.point and data.objects (array with exactly one polygon/plane name, e.g. ['ABC']). Do NOT use the key 'shape' for centroid.
INCENTER / ORTHOCENTER / CIRCUMCENTER: use data.point and data.shape (single triangle/polygon name string, e.g. 'ABC'). Do NOT use 'objects' for these three fact types.
INSCRIBED: data.inner is the NAME OF THE CENTER POINT to construct; data.outer is the NAME OF THE ENCLOSING SHAPE (triangle 'ABC' or solid 'S.ABCD'). Example: inner='I', outer='S.ABCD'.
CIRCUMSCRIBED: data.outer is the NAME OF THE CENTER POINT to construct; data.inner is the NAME OF THE ENCLOSED SHAPE. Example: outer='O', inner='S.ABCD'. Never swap these conventions with inscribed.
Do NOT use query type 'proof_existence' — it is not supported by the backend.
AREA / PERIMETER facts: use data.target (polygon name like 'ABC' or 'ABCD') and data.value (math expression string). Same format as length.
VOLUME fact: use data.target (solid name like 'S.ABCD' or 'SABC') and data.value. Do NOT confuse with query type 'volume'.
DISTANCE fact: use data.from, data.to, data.value. from/to can be point, segment, or plane names depending on the case.
EQUALITY fact: use data.objects as array of equal segments/objects (e.g. ['AB', 'CD'] or ['SA', 'SB', 'SC']).
COPLANAR fact: use data.points or data.objects listing all coplanar points/entities (at least 3).
COLLINEAR fact: use data.points listing collinear point names (at least 3).
TANGENT fact: use data.objects with exactly 2 items (plane/sphere, plane/circle, line/circle...) and data.point for tangent point when given.
QUERIES vs FACTS: Put questions (tính toán, chứng minh, phương trình) in queries[], not facts[].
QUERY shape: data.solid + data.plane for cross-section; or target starting with cross_section_.
QUERY intersection_line: data.planes or data.objects with exactly 2 plane names.
QUERY equation_line / equation_plane / equation_sphere / coordinates / locus: data.target required.
QUERY proof_parallel / proof_perpendicular / proof_equal: data.objects with 2 items.
QUERY cosine_between_planes: data.planes with 2 plane names.
QUERY sine_between_line_and_plane: data.objects = [segment, plane].
QUERY ratio_volume: data.solids with 2 solid names.
SHAPE regular_cube / regular_rectangular_cuboid / regular_parallelepiped: fact type 'shape' with matching shape enum and solid target like 'ABCD.A'B'C'D''.

SHAPE pentagon / hexagon: flat polygon targets 'ABCDE' or 'ABCDEF'. pentagonal_pyramid / hexagonal_pyramid: 'S.ABCDE' or 'S.ABCDEF'. pentagonal_prism / hexagonal_prism: 'ABCDE.A'B'C'D'E'' or 'ABCDEF.A'B'C'D'E'F''.
SHAPE regular_sphere: same as sphere — data.center and data.radius; use enum regular_sphere when the text emphasizes a standard sphere.

GENERIC SHAPES: Do not infer regular/equilateral/right/perpendicular special cases unless the problem text explicitly states them. 'hình chóp S.ABCD' alone -> shape pyramid only; do not add perpendicular SA to base unless stated.
PROJECTION: When text says H is orthogonal projection of S onto plane ABCD, emit projection fact { point: H, from: S, onto: ABCD } and include H in entities.points.
DECLARED POINTS ONLY: Every drawable point (M, P, I, H...) must appear in entities.points. Include entities.segments for line-only problems (e.g. AB).
